The military services have adopted generative AI tools to varying degrees and for a range of tasks — from tech support to finding files.","score":0.80698997,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://defensescoop.com/2025/07/14/pentagon-ai-contracts-musk-xai-google-openai-anthropic-cdao","title":"Pentagon awards mega contracts to Musk-owned company, other firms for new 'frontier AI' projects | DefenseScoop","content":"The Pentagon's Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office has awarded contracts to xAI, OpenAI, Anthropic and Google for the new effort. On the heels of an award to OpenAI for “frontier AI” projects, the Defense Department’s Chief Digital and Artificial Intelligence Office (CDAO) announced Monday that it has added three additional tech giants to the effort, including one owned by Elon Musk. Under the individual contracts — each worth up to $200 million — the Pentagon will have access to some of the most advanced AI capabilities developed by the four companies, including large language models, agentic AI workflows, cloud-based infrastructure and more.
